commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=23575b9854e2b1f28e8634c685928dcda5e0b25e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds DeepSeek provider-usage auth/fetching, summary-only usage snapshots, status/auth-status formatting, focused tests, and usage docs for showing DeepSeek account balance.

PR surface: Source +162, Tests +332, Docs +3, Generated 0. Total +497 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a broken existing behavior report. The PR body supplies redacted live terminal proof for the new DeepSeek fetch/format path, and unit tests cover mocked status integration.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide/accept the public provider-usage SDK contract, then the author or a repair worker can make the endpoint and docs fixes.

target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /v1/user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `3774b22e0f7df51d5af40deebe8751f10356ca2a`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/v1/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance ¥<redacted>
formatted=Balance ¥<redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal && pnpm lint:extensions:no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...touched TS files...`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: litang9

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(configure): migrate stale Codex defaults after OpenAI auth
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a configure/auth mismatch where OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex auth could preserve a stale legacy `codex/gpt-5.5` default while adding the canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` model.
- Keeps the selected primary model aligned with the canonical OpenAI provider id returned by auth.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #88456 reports successful ChatGPT/Codex device pairing followed by config returning to the menu with an unusable default model outside the selected provider model set.

What is the intended outcome?

- After OpenAI/Codex auth returns `openai/gpt-5.5`, a matching stale `codex/gpt-5.5` or `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5` instead of being preserved.
- Existing default preservation is unchanged for unrelated providers.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not redesign the OpenAI/Codex model picker or change live OAuth/device-code transport.
- This does not remove legacy model-map entries; it only prevents a matching stale legacy ref from remaining the primary default.

What does success look like?

- The configured primary model is present under the canonical OpenAI model entry after auth.
- Existing non-OpenAI defaults are still preserved when provider auth only adds another available model.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The preserve-existing-default guard in `src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts`.
- The regression test proving `codex/gpt-5.5` migrates to `openai/gpt-5.5` only for the matching OpenAI auth path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #88456

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #88456

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No direct maintainer request beyond the linked issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI/Codex auth no longer leaves a matching stale legacy Codex ref as the default primary while canonical OpenAI refs are added to the model map.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, invoking the actual provider-auth config application path with a redacted local token/provider setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the auth-choice config application path directly with before primary `codex/gpt-5.5`, selected auth model `openai/gpt-5.5`, and `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.cache/corepack PNPM_HOME=/home/node/.local/share/pnpm node --import tsx --input-type=module
  {
    "beforePrimary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
    "afterPrimary": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "allowlistKeys": [
      "codex/gpt-5.5",
      "openai/gpt-5.5"
    ],
    "note": {
      "title": "Model configured",
      "message": "Default model set to openai/gpt-5.5"
    }
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The primary default changes to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` while both legacy and canonical model entries remain present in `agents.defaults.models`.
- What was not tested: A full live ChatGPT/Codex device-code browser pairing against a real account from this container.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The worker cannot complete the interactive account authorization step without a user/browser handoff. The proof exercises the same config mutation path after auth returns a canonical OpenAI default.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The linked issue shows the broken state: primary `codex/gpt-5.5` with canonical OpenAI entries in the model allowlist.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.prompt-auth-config.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added provider-auth coverage for `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true` with existing primary `codex/gpt-5.5` and OpenAI auth default `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- The test verifies the primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5`, the canonical model entry is present, and the provider selection hook still runs.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The stale legacy default could be preserved as primary even though OpenAI auth added canonical `openai/*` entries, leaving the session default outside the selected provider allowlist.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; regression coverage was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- The preserve-existing-default behavior during provider auth.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The new exception is narrow: it only applies when OpenAI auth returns an `openai/*` model and the previous primary is a matching legacy `codex/*` or `openai-codex/*` ref for the same model id.
- Existing coverage still verifies unrelated existing defaults remain preserved.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI.
- Optional maintainer/live-environment confirmation of the full ChatGPT/Codex device-code prompt flow.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Initial Real behavior proof gate failure was addressed by replacing prose-only evidence with copied terminal output.
target.author: stevenepalmer
